notes: The definiens unfolds the bounded forms into unbounded quantifier + connective + membership: forall x in A phi := forall x (x in A -> phi) and exists x in A phi := exists x (x in A and phi). So implication, conjunction, and especially membership (in) are arguably constitutive (load-bearing) rather than ambient, since the abbreviation is precisely a translation into them. The author cited only the two quantifier forms, treating connectives/membership as ambient logical scaffolding -- a defensible convention. Flagged: if connectives/membership are cited elsewhere, def:membership (and the implication/conjunction nodes) belong here.
